About Ceres
Ceres is a leading developer of clean energy technology: fuel cells for power generation and electrolysers to produce green hydrogen. Its asset-light, licensing model has seen it establish partnerships with some of the world's largest companies, such as Doosan, Delta, Denso, Shell, Weichai and Thermax. Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy.
Read more on our website
www.ceres.tech
or follow us on
LinkedIn
.

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 12:00
RNS Number : 7522U
Ceres Power Holdings plc
27 February 2026
CWR.L
27 February 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres Power" or "the Company")
Total Voting Rights and admission of further securities to trading
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology,
confirms that as at 27 February 2026, the Company had 194,795,875 Ordinary Shares in issue, with one voting right each.
The Company does not hold any shares in treasury. The number of Ordinary Shares with voting rights is therefore 194,795,875.
Accordingly, these figures may be used by shareholders as a denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the Company, under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
In compliance with the Financial Conduct Authority's PRM Admission to Trading on a Regulated Market rules, the Company confirms that 83,679 shares allotted pursuant to the Company's share plans were admitted to trading over the calendar month to date. Information required in compliance with PRM 1.6.5 is detailed below:
Issuer name:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI):
213800N7AAHSXQUOA733
Regulated market on which the transferable securities have been admitted to trading:
Main Market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange
Name, type and ISIN of the shares:
Ordinary shares
GB00BG5KQW09
Number of further transferable securities admitted to trading covered by the notification:
83,679
Total number of transferable securities admitted to trading (taking into account the further issuance):
194,795,875
Confirmation that the further transferable securities admitted to trading are fungible with transferable securities already admitted to trading:
Confirmed.
Date range of securities admitted covered by the notification
1 February 2026 to 26 February 2026.

or contact:

Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy.

latest.title: Notice of Results
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0112U
Ceres Power Holdings plc
24 February 2026
CWR.L
24 February 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres", the "Company")
Notice of Results
Investor presentation via Investor Meet Company
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology, will announce its results for the year ended 31 December 2025 on Thursday, 26 March 2026.
Management will be hosting a live presentation relating to full year results via Investor Meet Company at 09:30 GMT.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted ahead of the event via the Investor Meet Company dashboard, or at any time during the live presentation. Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and register to attend via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/ceres-power-holdings-plc/register-investor
Ends
For further information visit
www.ceres.tech
or contact:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Merryl Black
Tel: +44(0)7770 853463
Email:
investors@cerespower.com
About Ceres
Ceres is a leading developer of clean energy technology: fuel cells for power generation and electrolysers to green hydrogen. Its asset-light, licensing model has seen it establish partnerships with some of the world's largest companies, such as Doosan, Delta, Denso, Shell, Weichai, and Thermax. Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking, and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy. company_30s.what_they_do: CWR Ltd supplies modular power‑electronics and battery‑storage solutions for industrial and renewable‑energy customers. The core products include silicon‑carbide converters, DC‑to‑DC chargers, and integrated storage modules that can be snapped together for rapid deployment.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global market for grid‑scale storage is expanding fast, driven by decarbonisation targets and the need for grid stability. CWR’s proprietary silicon‑carbide technology offers lower losses and higher power density than most competitors, giving it a niche edge in the UK and European markets.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a deep price slump after a string of negative earnings and a cash‑burn trajectory that has forced a restructuring of its debt. Recent filings show a loss and a reduction in institutional holdings. The share trades at a fraction of its pre‑pandemic valuation, signalling a steep discount to the underlying assets.

rns_narrative: CWR released its latest results, confirming a loss and a need for additional financing. The company highlighted a shift in focus toward larger OEM contracts, suggesting a pivot toward higher‑margin projects.

A new block‑listing interim review was announced, indicating that regulators are scrutinising the company’s governance and shareholder structure. This could either trigger a positive clarification or a negative ruling.
The company disclosed changes to its voting‑rights structure, hinting at potential dilution or a planned equity raise to shore up its balance sheet.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious – low chatter with a few hopeful voices.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to a potential partnership with a major OEM and a forthcoming funding round as catalysts that could lift the stock.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others fear continued cash burn, regulatory delays, and a lack of clear revenue streams, keeping the price stuck near the bottom.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has been flat but is slowly leaning slightly bullish after a handful of positive posts about upcoming tech announcements.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, signalling maximum surprise potential when a catalyst finally arrives.

near_term_catalysts: A positive earnings call that shows a turnaround in cash flow or a new OEM deal announcement.
A regulatory decision on the block‑listing review that clears CWR for continued trading.
A volume spike at the support level around £0.02, indicating capitulation is over.
A funding announcement or a convertible‑bond issue that injects fresh capital.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear volume uptick that exceeds 2× the 20‑day average and a break above the 20‑day moving average. A bullish crossover on the 10‑day and 50‑day lines would also support the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score of 8.0 shows a tight compression, suggesting the stock is in the late stage of a deep crash. A bounce would need to be confirmed by volume and a sustained move above the 20‑day MA.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the price recovers to roughly the halfway point between the current bottom and the pre‑crash peak, the upside is about 45% from the current level. Historically, a GREEN signal delivers ~40% average return, so the mean‑reversion thesis is strong. The deep crash (70‑95% down) creates a high‑reward asymmetry that is typical of this system’s best performers.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Continued cash burn could keep the price depressed, and regulatory uncertainty might delay any catalyst. The realistic worst‑case is a further 10% drop before a rebound, which would still leave a modest upside if the bounce occurs.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside 45% versus a downside of 10% from the current bottom gives a 4.5:1 risk‑reward ratio. Even a 20% rebound would produce a 2:1 payoff.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when price breaks above the 20‑day moving average with volume >2× the 20‑day average. Place a stop 10% below entry. Size the position at 5% of total capital, adjusting for volatility.
playbook.if_watching: Hold off until you see either a clear volume spike at the £0.02 support level or a positive earnings announcement. Once one of those occurs, evaluate the move and consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: No genuine terminal risk identified. The deep crash and lack of delisting signals keep the setup viable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: CWR’s steep crash and silent market make it a prime contrarian play that could double in value if a timely catalyst triggers a mean‑reversion bounce, offering a roughly 4‑to‑1 reward with modest downside.
